title: Every required field populates required_message with a brief instruction
description: When required=yes is set on a survey row, populate required_message with a brief instruction telling the end user what to enter. OpenClinica displays this inline when the user tries to submit without completing the field. Format options: 'Please indicate <field description>.', '<Field name> is required.', 'Please enter the <field description>.'. Apply universally — every required survey row regardless of type (date, select_one, select_multiple, integer, decimal, text).
rationale: OpenClinica's empty-required-field error is generic by default ('This field is required'). For forms with multiple required fields, the generic message doesn't tell the data-entry user WHICH field was missed. A populated required_message reframes the error inline at the right field with field-specific guidance, dramatically reducing submission rejection rates and operator frustration. Universal application keeps the operator experience consistent across forms.

flag.message: Required field ${field.name} on ${form.form_id} must populate required_message. Format options: 'Please indicate <description>.', '<Field name> is required.', 'Please enter the <description>.'

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §6 during Phase B.1d. Structured: applies_when checks field.required, effect uses require (raises flag if path is empty). Message content is left to convention authors (per the format options in description) rather than being auto-set by ensure, because the right message text depends on the field's purpose (date vs select vs text vs integer).
